splash.body: The basic workflow for creating an order in Slingshot is pretty straightforward for a performance order. Let's try to figure out the core steps in the process!
Let's learn about third party media plans. Click each step of the workflow to uncover more details.

conclusion.body: Nice job! Building performance orders isn't too bad, when you know who handles what. In the next few lessons, let's see how to build different types of orders, as well as how to build a pitch deck.

items.title: Step 1: Create Media Plan and Pull Avails in Slingshot
items.body: Account executives and sales planners plan out line items and pull the avails. That's how you figure out the percentage of inventory that's still available. Work with your pod to recommend revisions. Afterwards account executives and sales planners make the appropriate revisions to the plan.

items.title: Step 2: Revise Media Plan and Re-Pull Avails with YieldEx
items.body: Some line items in your plan might not have enough impression inventory for your budget. It happens! Revise these line items and re-pull the impressions using YieldEx. You might need to revise your media plan a few times. Afterwards, the account executive sends it to the client. Media plan revisions can also occur after client feedback, which follows a similar process.

items.title: Step 3: Make the Third Party Media Plan
items.body: After everyone's signed the insertion order, sales planners and account managers may need to recheck impression inventory. This is to make sure that the inventory is still available.
